lede: A look at March 6 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Michelangelo and Gabriel García Márquez, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: MS Herald of Free Enterprise
events.desc: The British ferry MS Herald of Free Enterprise capsizes in about 90 seconds, killing 193.

events.title: Joseph Stalin
events.desc: Cold War: Joseph Stalin's daughter Svetlana Alliluyeva defects to the United States.

events.title: Nation of Islam
events.desc: Nation of Islam leader Elijah Muhammad officially gives boxing champion Cassius Clay the name Muhammad Ali.

events.title: Ghana
events.desc: Ghana becomes the first Sub-Saharan country to gain independence from the British.

events.title: Cologne
events.desc: World War II: Cologne is captured by American troops. On the same day, Operation Spring Awakening, the last major German offensive of the war, begins.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Generalfeldmarschall Erwin Rommel launches the Battle of Medenine in an attempt to slow down the British Eighth Army. It fails, and he leaves Africa three days later.

events.title: Scottish National Antarctic Expedition
events.desc: Scottish National Antarctic Expedition: Led by William Speirs Bruce, the Antarctic region of Coats Land is discovered from the Scotia.

events.title: Wilhelm II
events.desc: An anarchist assassin tries to kill German Emperor Wilhelm II.

events.title: Texas Revolution
events.desc: Texas Revolution: Battle of the Alamo: After a thirteen-day siege by an army of 3,000 Mexican troops, the 187 Texas volunteers, including frontiersman Davy Crockett and colonel Jim Bowie, defending the Alamo are killed and the fort is captured.

events.title: 1447 conclave
events.desc: Election of Pope Nicholas V following the death of Pope Eugene IV on 23 February 1447.

births.name: Gabriel García Márquez
births.line: Colombian journalist and author, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Jürgen Stroop, German SS general, and executed war criminal
Ferdinand von Lindemann, German mathematician and academic
Gottlieb Daimler, German engineer and businessman, co-founded Daimler-Motoren-Gesellschaft

observances.text: Christian feast day: Kyneburga, Kyneswide and Tibba
European Day of the Righteous, commemorates those who have stood up against crimes against humanity and totalitarianism with their own moral responsibility. (Europe)
Norfolk Island Foundation Day, the founding of Norfolk Island in 1788.
